title: projectpr1d_metab.nan
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"50qeo","text":"This is a protocol for running the Bruker pulse program \"projectpr1d\".","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"66ct6","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"c9mhc","text":"This pulse program was originally proposed in:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"degrk","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"818ku","text":" ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"cg4nn","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"citation","mutability":"IMMUTABLE","data":{"guid":"FBFF1D4789E94FA4B25051B1FFED186A","title":"Alternatives to Nuclear Overhauser Enhancement Spectroscopy Presat and Carr-Purcell-Meiboom-Gill Presat for NMR-Based Metabolomics.","protocol_guid":"","journal":"","date":"2017-09-05T00:00:00Z","created_on":"2026-02-10T22:33:37Z","doi":"https://doi.org/10.1021/acs.analchem.7b02354","authors":"Le Guennec A, Tayyari F, Edison AS","can_edit":false}}}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"3i9ah","text":"This protocol intends to provide concise instructions to carry out the experiment. For more comprehensive information, see Bruker's documentation including \"Basic NMR Experiments\" by clicking ? →  Manuals (docs) on the menu bar on TopSpin.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":192,"length":1},{"style":"bold","offset":197,"length":14}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"3vmkh","text":"This protocol assumes your sample is loaded, locked, tuned, and shimmed in the magnet.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: On the menu bar on TopSpin, click on Start → Create Dataset (This protocol uses TopSpin 3.6.4, and the interface may look different on other TopSpin versions.) You can also use the new command in the command line to do this step.
When Lock, Tune, Shim are complete proceed to the specific protocol for your desired pulseprogram(s).

A new window opens. On the right top bar, select Source = /opt/NAN_METAB/par In the list, select the one you want to use: PROJECTPR1D_br600_serum.par : Parameter set optimized for serum and plasma samples. Parameter set names in the list vary between spectrometers (e.g., PROJECTPR1D_br800_serum.par). Click OK

Enter NAME : Name of a set of datasets (e.g., human_serum_study1). Use a single string. EXPNO : Dataset number. Use a positive integer. Select Directory : Your directory. Your new dataset will be stored in Directory / NAME / EXPNO
Select Read parameterset Click the button Select
pulsecal
rga
zg
Go to the " USE DEFAULT " tab below to proceed with the default optimized parameters.

Load the calibrated P1 using the following command in the command line. getprosol 1H [calibrated P1 value] [power level for P1] (e.g., getprosol 1H 10.01 -7.45) [power level for P1] varies between spectrometers. Never use a wrong [power level for P1]
Select the AcquPars tab to show a parameter set. You can also use the ased command in the command line.

Common additional parameters to adjust: Abbreviation Parameter Note 1 TD Size of FID To optimize the digital resolution. 2 SW Spectral width To minimize unnecessary end regions 3 AQ Acquisition time 1–3 seconds is adequate. Changing TD or SW will also change AQ 4 DS Number of dummy scans To allow the system to achieve a steady state. At least ≥16 5 D2 Time period (τ) in the T2 filter To optimize the echo time 6 L4 Number of loops in the T2 filter To optimize the echo time 7 PLW9 Level of attenuation used for the presaturation pulses To optimize the water suppression. DO NOT reduce the level below the default (~45–55 dB). There are two columns, and the second column is for dB.
Click on Acquire → Gain in the menu bar to automatically set the receiver gain. You can also use the rga command in the command line.
Click Go in the menu bar to acquire a spectrum. You can also use the zg command in the command line.
Click on Process → Proc. Spectrum in the menu bar to execute an automated processing macro.
Click the icon _∏ _ to see a complete parameter set. You can also use the eda command in the command line.
Acquire a spectrum. If more optimization is needed, go to step #3.3 .
Click OK
If you want to modify parameters to improve your spectrum, go to step #2 and move to a step case "MODIFY PAR".
Change parameters as needed. Typically, the main parameter to adjust is NS: Abbreviation Parameter Note 1 NS Number of scans To increase the signal-to-noise ratio (S/N). S/N increases as √NS
creator: NAN-KB UGA
